ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch appeared by zoom for and with Debra Dolch by zoom.

The Court noted that no other parties are appearing and that it is satisfied with the investment review authority. Mr. Urbatsch and Ms. Dolch stated that they have nothing to add. The Sixth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-13-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch appeared by telephone for and with Debra Dolch (present).

The Court stated that it is confused about the invoices for the accountant (6.25 hours for 8/15/17 and 4.29 hours for 8/16/17) and questions whether this is a combination of the Accountant's hours and Ms. Dolch's hours. Ms. Dolch stated that it is a combination of her time and the Accountant's time, that up until recently she charged a percentage of the work for Trustee, that the Court recently has changed its policy, that her new practice is to pay the third party and keep track of her time, and that she is requesting that her fees be reduced. The Fifth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Continued To Mar-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-11-2019 Continued To Feb-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).

Mr. Urbatsch stated that the purchase of the second van was done without prior Court approval due to the need being immediate, that the Trustee has discretion to make distribution for care and the van, and that there is no legal requirement to do so. Mr. Urbatsch also stated that the Court has jurisdiction not supervision, that they do not have to come in and seek permission, that they exercised discretion in between accounting periods and did not want additional expenses, that it is their belief that the ETF's met with Probate Code 24, that it is less than 5% of the assets, and that they have been liquidated and reinvested. The Court informed Counsel that it expects the parties to obtain prior approval. The Fourth Report and Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: kevin Urbatsch for and with Debra Dolch (Trustee).

Mr. Urbatsch stated that the bank statements had been filed. In addition, Mr. Urbatsch stated that the person doing the renovation work was Darlene's Husband, that Debra Dolch has no affiliate realtionship with him, that he was selected because of his skills, that it a was a lot less expensive to have him do the work and that it was in the Trust's best interest to use him. Furthermore, he stated that they hired the Case Manager to deal with medical coverage issues. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.
